subject: science
question: Define catenation.
answer: The property of self-linking of atoms of an element through covalent bonds in order to form straight chain, branched chains or cyclic chains of different sizes is called catenation.

subject: science
question: How does valency of an element vary across a period?
answer: The valency of an element first increases and then decreases across a period.
topic: Trends in the Modern Periodic Table

subject: science
question: State the principle behind electric generator. [Based on a paragraph about Muppandal wind farm in Tamil Nadu, India's fifth largest wind-power producer, producing AC at 240 V, 50 Hz.]
answer: The principle behind electric generator is Electromagnetic Induction — the phenomenon of producing current in a coil by changing the magnetic field associated with it.

subject: science
question: The output frequency of wind turbine is 50 Hz. What is meant by this statement? [Based on the Muppandal wind farm passage.]
answer: The polarity of the output alternating current changes every 1/100 seconds. (Alternatively: In 1 second the output AC completes 50 cycles.)

subject: science
question: Why do you think Muppandal is at an advantageous position for this project? [Based on the Muppandal wind farm passage.]
answer: The suitability of Muppandal as a site for wind farms stems from its geographical location as it has access to the seasonal monsoon winds.

subject: science
question: Based on the data represented in the graph below, which of the two cities A or B would be an ideal location for establishing a wind-farm and why? [Graph shows wind speed data for cities A and B throughout the year.]
answer: City A. It is more suitable for a wind-farm as there is consistently high wind-speed in that city throughout the year.

subject: science
question: Refer to Table B showing the blood report of the levels of glucose of patients X and Y. Infer the disease which can be diagnosed from the given data. [Table B gives blood glucose ranges for patients X and Y at various times of day.]
answer: Diabetes

subject: science
question: Identify the hormone whose level in the blood is responsible for the above disease (diabetes). [Based on blood glucose data tables.]
answer: Insulin

subject: science
question:
Which one of the following diets would you recommend to the affected patient (with diabetes)?
i) High sugar and low fat diet.
ii) Low sugar and high protein diet.
iii) High Fat and low fiber diet.
iv) Low sugar and high fiber diet.
options: High sugar and low fat diet.
Low sugar and high protein diet.
High Fat and low fiber diet.
Low sugar and high fiber diet.
answer: iv) Low sugar and high fiber diet.

subject: science
question:
Refer to Table A and suggest the value of the mean blood glucose level beyond which doctor's advice is necessary:
i) 180 mg/dL
ii) 115 mg/dL
iii) 50 mg/dL
iv) 80 mg/dL
answer: i) 180 mg/dL

subject: science
question:
When we enter a dark room coming from outside, immediately the things inside the room do not appear clear to our eyes. This is because
i) pupils do not open at all in the dark.
ii) pupils take time to adjust.
iii) light travels slower in a dark room.
iv) pupils open very quickly in the dark.
OR
The phenomena of light responsible for the working of the human eye is
i) reflection
ii) refraction
iii) power of accommodation
iv) persistence of vision
options: pupils do not open at all in the dark.
light travels slower in a dark room.
pupils open very quickly in the dark.
answer:
ii) pupils take time to adjust.
OR
ii) refraction

subject: science
question:
When a 4 V battery is connected across an unknown resistor there is a current of 100 mA in the circuit. The value of the resistance of the resistor is:
i) 4 Ω
ii) 40 Ω
iii) 400 Ω
iv) 0.4 Ω

subject: science
question:
Unit of electric power may also be expressed as:
i) volt-ampere
ii) kilowatt-hour
iii) watt-second
iv) joule-second
answer: i) volt-ampere. (Power = Voltage × Current)

subject: science
question:
It was found that water from a river was contaminated with Coliform bacteria. Which one of the following pollutants might have got mixed with the water?
i) Fertilizer run off
ii) Industrial waste
iii) Pesticides
iv) Human faecal matter
OR
Which one of the following stakeholders of forests causes the maximum damage to forest?
i) People who live in or around the forest
ii) The forest department of the government
iii) The wildlife and native enthusiasts
iv) The industrialists
options: OR: People who live in or around the forest
OR: The forest department of the government
OR: The wildlife and native enthusiasts
answer:
iv) Human faecal matter
OR
iv) The industrialists
chapter: Our Environment / Management of Natural Resources

subject: science
question:
Which one of the following greenhouse gases is a contributor due to incomplete combustion of coal and petroleum?
i) Oxides of nitrogen
ii) Methane
iii) Carbon monoxide
iv) Carbon dioxide
answer: iii) Carbon monoxide

subject: science
question:
Which of the following reactions is an endothermic reaction?
i) Burning of coal.
ii) Decomposition of vegetable matter into compost.
iii) Process of respiration.
iv) Decomposition of calcium carbonate to form quick lime and carbon dioxide.
options: Decomposition of vegetable matter into compost.
Decomposition of calcium carbonate to form quick lime and carbon dioxide.
answer: iv) Decomposition of calcium carbonate to form quick lime and carbon dioxide.
topic: Types of Chemical Reactions / Endothermic Reactions

subject: science
question:
Identify the basic salt from the following salts:
i) Na₂CO₃
ii) NH₄Cl
iii) NaNO₃
iv) KCl
answer: i) Na₂CO₃

subject: science
question:
The positions of four elements A, B, C and D in the modern periodic table are shown below (A is in the top row, B is below A in the same group; C is to the right of A in the same period, D is below C in the same group). Which element is most likely to form an acidic oxide?
i) A
ii) B
iii) C
iv) D
OR
Elements P, Q, R and S have atomic numbers 11, 15, 17 and 18 respectively. Which of them are reactive non-metals?
i) P and Q
ii) P and R
iii) Q and R
iv) R and S
answer:
iii) C
OR
iii) Q and R

subject: science
question:
Assertion (A): Two given structures are structural isomers of butane.
Reason (R): Structural isomers have the same molecular formula but they differ in their structures.
[Select the correct answer from: i) Both A and R are true and R is correct explanation; ii) Both A and R are true but R is not correct explanation; iii) A is true but R is false; iv) A is false but R is true.]
options: Both A and R are true and R is correct explanation of the assertion.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of the assertion.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
answer: i) Both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

subject: science
question:
Assertion (A): A fuse wire is always connected in parallel with the mainline.
Reason (R): If a current larger than the specified value flows through the circuit, fuse wire melts.
[Select the correct answer from: i) Both A and R are true and R is correct explanation; ii) Both A and R are true but R is not correct explanation; iii) A is true but R is false; iv) A is false but R is true.]
options: Both A and R are true and R is correct explanation of the assertion.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of the assertion.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
answer: iv) Assertion is false but reason is true. (Fuse wire is connected in series, not parallel.)

subject: science
question:
(i) Write two observations when lead nitrate is heated in a test tube.
(ii) Name the type of reaction.
(iii) Write a balanced chemical equation to represent the above reaction.
answer:
(i) It turns yellow due to formation of lead oxide and reddish brown fumes evolve.
(ii) Thermal decomposition reaction.
(iii) 2Pb(NO₃)₂ →(heat) 2PbO + 4NO₂ + O₂

subject: science
question:
A compound 'X' of sodium is used as an antacid and it decomposes on strong heating.
(i) Name the compound 'X' and give its chemical formula.
(ii) Write a balanced chemical equation to represent the decomposition of 'X'.
(iii) Give one use of compound 'X' besides an antacid.
OR
You are provided with 90 mL of distilled water and 10 mL of concentrated sulphuric acid to prepare dilute sulphuric acid.
(i) What is the correct way of preparing dilute sulphuric acid? Give reason.
(ii) How will the concentration of H₃O⁺ ions change on dilution?
answer:
(i) Sodium bicarbonate / Sodium hydrogencarbonate / baking soda; formula NaHCO₃.
(ii) 2NaHCO₃ →(heat) Na₂CO₃ + CO₂ + H₂O
(iii) Used in fire extinguisher / for baking.
OR
(i) Add 10 mL of concentrated sulphuric acid slowly to 90 mL of water with constant stirring. Reason: Dilution is highly exothermic; adding water to acid causes splashing.
(ii) Concentration of H₃O⁺ ions decreases per unit volume.

subject: science
question: Two elements X and Y have atomic numbers 12 and 16 respectively. To which period of the modern periodic table do these two elements belong? What type of bond will be formed between them and why? Also give the chemical formula of the compound formed.
answer: X (at. no. 12): 2,8,2; Y (at. no. 16): 2,8,6. Both belong to the 3rd period. Ionic bond will be formed because X will lose 2 electrons and Y will gain 2 electrons to complete their octets. Formula of compound: XY (MgS).

subject: science
question:
(i) Create a terrestrial food chain depicting four trophic levels.
(ii) Why do we not find food chains of more than four trophic levels in nature?
OR
How will you create an artificial aquatic ecosystem, which is self-sustainable?
answer:
(i) Any valid terrestrial food chain with 4 trophic levels e.g. Grass → Grasshopper → Frog → Snake (or similar).
(ii) According to the 10% law, the amount of energy available will not be sufficient for the survival of the organism in the 5th trophic level.
OR
Large jar filled with water, oxygen, food and aquatic plants and animals. Include oxygen pump, fish food, aquatic plants (producers provide O₂ during photosynthesis), aquatic animals (consumers release CO₂ for photosynthesis), and decomposers for natural cleaning.

subject: science
question: Explain the processes of aerobic respiration in mitochondria of a cell and anaerobic respiration in yeast and muscle with the help of word equations.
answer:
Aerobic respiration (in mitochondria): Glucose + Oxygen → Carbon dioxide + Water + Energy (ATP).
Anaerobic respiration in yeast: Glucose → Ethanol + Carbon dioxide + Energy.
Anaerobic respiration in muscle: Glucose → Lactic acid + Energy.

subject: science
question: In a pea plant, the trait of flowers bearing purple colour (PP) is dominant over white colour (pp). Explain the inheritance pattern of F₁ and F₂ generations with the help of a cross following the rules of inheritance of traits. State the visible characters of F₁ and F₂ progenies.
answer: Parental cross: PP × pp → F₁: all Pp (all purple). F₁ selfing: Pp × Pp → F₂: PP : 2Pp : pp = 3 purple : 1 white. Visible characters: F₁ — all flowers are purple coloured; F₂ — 3 are purple coloured and 1 is white coloured.

subject: science
question: Explain giving reasons the bending of the shoot tip of a plant towards light source coming from one side of the plant.
answer: When growing plant shoot tip detects light, a hormone called auxin is synthesised in the shoot tip which is sensitive to light. Auxin diffuses towards the shady side of the stem. It stimulates the growth of cells on the shady side of the plant which causes the shoot to bend towards the light source. This gives the appearance that the stem bends in the direction of light.

subject: science
question:
It is desired to obtain an erect image of an object, using concave mirror of focal length of 12 cm.
(i) What should be the range of the object distance in the above case?
(ii) Will the image be smaller or larger than the object? Draw a ray diagram to show the formation of image in this case.
(iii) Where will the image of this object be, if it is placed 24 cm in front of the mirror?
answer:
(i) Range of object distance should be 0 cm to less than 12 cm (i.e., object must be between the pole and focus).
(ii) The image will be larger than the object (virtual, erect, magnified image formed behind the mirror).
(iii) Image will be at 24 cm in front of the mirror (i.e., the image is formed at C when object is at C for concave mirror — using mirror formula: 1/v + 1/u = 1/f; u = −24, f = −12 ⇒ v = −24 cm).

subject: science
question: Suppose your parents have constructed a two room house and you want that in the living room there should be a provision of one electric bulb, one electric fan, a refrigerator and a plug point for appliances of power up to 2 kilowatt. Draw a circuit diagram showing electric fuse and earthing as safety devices.
answer: Circuit diagram with: four components (bulb, fan, refrigerator, plug point) connected in parallel; fuse connected in series in the main line; earthing shown for appliances. All components should be labelled. Live and earth wires must be shown.

subject: science
question:
In the figure given below, a narrow beam of white light is shown to pass through a triangular glass prism. After passing through the prism, it produces a spectrum XY on the screen.
(i) Name the phenomenon.
(ii) State the colours seen at X and Y.
(iii) Why do different colours of white light bend at different angles through a prism?
OR
(i) What is visible spectrum?
(ii) Why is red used as the stopping light at traffic signals?
(iii) Two triangular glass prisms are kept together connected through their rectangular side. A light beam is passed through one side of the combination. Will there be any dispersion? Justify your answer.
answer:
(i) Dispersion.
(ii) X — Violet; Y — Red.
(iii) Different colours of white light bend through different angles with respect to the incident beam due to difference in speed of light of different wavelengths.
OR
(i) Visible spectrum is the band of coloured components of a white light beam.
(ii) Red light is scattered the least by air molecules and has longer wavelength, so it travels the longest distance.
(iii) No dispersion. The given setup behaves like a glass slab, resulting in recombination of the seven colours to produce white light.

subject: science
question:
Metal X is found in nature as its sulphide XS. It is used in the galvanisation of iron articles. Identify the metal X. How will you convert this sulphide ore into the metal? Explain with equations.
OR
State the reason for the following:
(i) Aluminium oxide is called an amphoteric oxide.
(ii) An iron strip dipped in a blue copper sulphate solution turns the blue solution pale green.
(iii) Hydrogen gas is not evolved when most metals react with nitric acid.
(iv) Calcium does not occur in free state in nature.
(v) Sodium or potassium metals are kept immersed under kerosene.
answer:
Metal X is Zinc. The sulphide ore is first roasted (heated in supply of oxygen) to convert it to oxide: 2ZnS + 3O₂ →(heat) 2ZnO + 2SO₂. Zinc oxide is then reduced by heating with carbon: 2ZnO + C → 2Zn + CO₂.
OR
(i) Aluminium oxide reacts with both acids and bases to form salts, hence it is amphoteric.
(ii) Iron being more reactive than copper displaces copper from copper sulphate to form green ferrous sulphate solution.
(iii) Nitric acid is a strong oxidising agent; hydrogen gas produced gets oxidised to H₂O.
(iv) Calcium is very reactive; it reacts with chemicals in surroundings and occurs only in combined state.
(v) Sodium and potassium are highly reactive metals that react vigorously with oxygen in air and may catch fire; they do not react with kerosene.

subject: science
question:
The formulae of four organic compounds are given below:
A: C₂H₄, B: CH₃COOH, C: C₂H₅OH, D: C₂H₆
(i) Which one of these compounds A, B, C or D is a saturated hydrocarbon?
(ii) Identify the organic acid and give its structural formula.
(iii) Which of the above compounds when heated at 443 K in the presence of concentrated H₂SO₄ forms ethene as the major product? What is the role played by concentrated H₂SO₄ in this reaction? Also write the chemical equation involved.
(iv) Give a chemical equation when B and C react with each other in presence of concentrated H₂SO₄. Name the major product formed and mention one of its important uses.
answer:
(i) D (C₂H₆) is a saturated hydrocarbon.
(ii) B (CH₃COOH) is the organic acid. Structural formula: H₃C−COOH (CH₃COOH).
(iii) C (C₂H₅OH). H₂SO₄ acts as a dehydrating agent and removes a water molecule from ethanol. C₂H₅OH →(hot conc. H₂SO₄, 443 K) C₂H₄ + H₂O.
(iv) CH₃COOH + C₂H₅OH ⇌(conc. H₂SO₄) CH₃COOC₂H₅ + H₂O. Major product is Ester; used in making perfumes / flavouring agents.

subject: science
question:
(i) Identify any two parts from the given diagram of the human heart which carry oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.
(ii) Explain the process of double circulation with the help of a flow chart.
answer:
(i) Oxygenated blood: Left ventricle / Aorta / Left auricle / Pulmonary vein (any two). Deoxygenated blood: Right ventricle / Pulmonary artery / Right auricle / Vena cava (any two).
(ii) Double circulation flow chart: Body → Vena cava → Right auricle → Right ventricle → Pulmonary artery → Lungs → Pulmonary vein → Left auricle → Left ventricle → Aorta → Body (pulmonary + systemic circulation).

subject: science
question:
(i) Describe the various steps involved in the process of binary fission with the help of a diagram.
(ii) Why do multicellular organisms use complex way of reproduction?
OR
(i) Describe the role of prostate gland, seminal vesicle and testes in the human male reproductive system.
(ii) How is the surgical removal of unwanted pregnancies misused?
(iii) Explain the role of oral contraceptive pills in preventing conception.
answer:
(i) Binary fission steps: nucleus elongates and divides → cell elongates → cell constricts in the middle → two daughter cells formed, each with a nucleus. Diagram should show the above stages.
(ii) Multicellular organisms cannot reproduce by simple cell division because they are not simple random collections of cells. Specialized cells are organized as tissues and organs. Cell-by-cell division would be impractical, hence complex reproduction methods are required.
OR
(i) Prostate gland and seminal vesicle add secretions so sperms are in a fluid, making transport easier and providing nutrition. Testes secrete testosterone which brings about pubertal changes in boys.
(ii) Surgical removal is misused for female foeticide / illegal sex-selective abortion.
(iii) Oral contraceptive pills interfere with the release of eggs so eggs are not released.

subject: science
question:
(i) Consider a conductor of resistance 'R', length 'L', thickness 'd' and resistivity 'ρ'. Now this conductor is cut into four equal parts. What will be the new resistivity of each of these parts? Why?
(ii) Find the resistance if all of these parts are connected in:
(a) Parallel
(b) Series
(iii) Out of the combinations of resistors mentioned above in the previous part, for a given voltage which combination will consume more power and why?
answer:
(i) Resistivity will not change as it depends on the nature of the material of the conductor (remains ρ).
(ii) Length of each part = L/4. Resistance of each part = ρ(L/4)/A = R/4.
(a) In parallel: 1/R_eq = 4/(R/4) = 16/R ⇒ R_eq = R/16 Ω.
(b) In series: R_eq = 4 × R/4 = R Ω.
(iii) P = V²/R. If R_eq is less, power consumed is more. R_eq is lesser in the parallel combination, so parallel combination consumes more power.
topic: Resistance / Series and Parallel / Electric Power

subject: science
question:
(i) A lens produces a magnification of −0.5. Is this a converging or diverging lens? If the focal length of the lens is 6 cm, draw a ray diagram showing the image formation in this case.
(ii) A girl was playing with a thin beam of light from a laser torch by directing it from different directions on a convex lens held vertically. She was surprised to see that in a particular direction, the beam of light continues to move along the same direction after passing through the lens. State the reason for her observation. Draw a ray diagram to support your answer.
OR
(i) On entering in a medium from air, the speed of light becomes half of its value in air. Find the refractive index of that medium with respect to air.
(ii) A glass slab made of a material of refractive index n₁ is kept in a medium of refractive index n₂. A light ray is incident on the slab. Draw the path of the rays of light emerging from the glass slab, if (i) n₁ > n₂ (ii) n₁ = n₂ (iii) n₁ < n₂.
answer:
(i) Since magnification is negative (−0.5), the image is real and inverted. A converging (convex) lens can produce a real and inverted image. Ray diagram: object beyond 2F, image between F and 2F on other side.
(ii) The girl must have directed the ray along the direction of the optical centre of the lens. A ray passing through the optical centre passes straight without deviation.
OR
(i) µ = velocity in air / velocity in medium = v/(v/2) = 2.
(ii) (a) n₁ > n₂: ray bends towards normal on entering, then away from normal on emerging — exits parallel to incident ray but displaced. (b) n₁ = n₂: ray passes straight through undeviated. (c) n₁ < n₂: ray bends away from normal on entering, then towards normal on emerging — exits parallel to incident ray but displaced.
